  "description": "Nkoyo Pharmaceuticals Limited is a Nigerian company founded over three decades ago as a simple open market drug trade outlet but has over the years evolved into a full-fledged pharmaceutical company run by a team of professionals with its corporate headquarter in Lagos, Nigeria.

Our focus is mainly on delivering quality pharmaceutical products to our customers, distributors and stakeholders within and outside the country.&nbsp;We specialize in the wholesale distribution of quality pharmaceutical products and operate as the sole distributor in Africa for major pharmaceutical production companies in India.&nbsp;We are a leading contract distributor for Pharmaceutical Tablets, Gels, Pain Relief Balms, Creams, Sex Enhancers etc. Our Products are manufactured at factories with WHO Certification and are distributed in more than 10 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR / Admin Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
Human Resources Support:


	Maintain HR database (HRIS) and employee records (paper and electronic).
	Coordinate the onboarding process: new hire paperwork, contracts, orientation, and setting up workstations.
	Support recruitment: posting jobs, screening resumes, and scheduling interviews.
	Assist with payroll by keeping records of attendance, leave, and overtime.
	Help administer employee benefits and address basic HR inquiries.


Administrative Support:


	Manage office supplies, mail, and correspondence.
	Schedule meetings, events, and manage calendars for the HR team.
	Process company expenses and maintain office files.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Experience: Proven experience as an HR Assistant, Staff Assistant, or relevant administrative role.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Organization: Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to multitask.
	Confidentiality: High level of integrity and ability to handle confidential information.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp; to:&nbsp;jobs@nkoyopharma.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

HR / Admin Officer Job at Nkoyo Pharmaceuticals Limited




        
    

        
        Nkoyo Pharmaceuticals Limited is a Nigerian company founded over three decades ago as a simple open market drug trade outlet but has over the years evolved into a full-fledged pharmaceutical company run by a team of professionals with its corporate headquarter in Lagos, Nigeria.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our focus is mainly on delivering quality pharmaceutical products to our customers, distributors and stakeholders within and outside the country. We specialize in the wholesale distribution of quality pharmaceutical products and operate as the sole distributor in Africa for major pharmaceutical production companies in India. We are a leading contract distributor for Pharmaceutical Tablets, Gels, Pain Relief Balms, Creams, Sex Enhancers etc. Our Products are manufactured at factories with WHO Certification and are distributed in more than 10 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR / Admin Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
Human Resources Support:


	Maintain HR database (HRIS) and employee records (paper and electronic).
	Coordinate the onboarding process: new hire paperwork, contracts, orientation, and setting up workstations.
	Support recruitment: posting jobs, screening resumes, and scheduling interviews.
	Assist with payroll by keeping records of attendance, leave, and overtime.
	Help administer employee benefits and address basic HR inquiries.


Administrative Support:


	Manage office supplies, mail, and correspondence.
	Schedule meetings, events, and manage calendars for the HR team.
	Process company expenses and maintain office files.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Experience: Proven experience as an HR Assistant, Staff Assistant, or relevant administrative role.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Organization: Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to multitask.
	Confidentiality: High level of integrity and ability to handle confidential information.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV  to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "title": "Technical Sales Engineer",
  "description": "Brainshare Technologies &amp; Services Nig. Ltd is a Telecom services provider. We offer connectivity, managed services and other value added services through our fiber optic infrastructure.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Sales Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Technical Sales Engineer will provide technical support and expertise during client engagements, working closely with the Sales &amp; Business Development Executive to drive successful sales outcomes.
	The role involves understanding customer requirements, presenting technical solutions, and ensuring alignment with company telecoms and cloud service offerings.


Key Responsibilities
Pre-Sales Support:


	Collaborate with the Sales/Business Development Executive to understand client needs and tailor technical solutions.
	Prepare technical proposals, solution designs, and presentations.
	Conduct product demonstrations for telecom and cloud-based services.


Client Engagement:


	Accompany the Sales/Business Development Executive to client meetings.
	Clearly articulate technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
	Provide expert advice on telecom infrastructure, cloud services, and related solutions.


Solution Design &amp; Implementation Support:


	Design scalable and cost-effective solutions based on customer requirements.
	Liaise with internal technical teams to ensure feasibility and proper deployment.
	Support initial implementation and onboarding processes where required.


Product Knowledge &amp; Advisory:
Maintain deep knowledge of company offerings, including:


	Cloud services (IaaS, SaaS, Direct Connect, backups, hosting).
	Internet services (fiber, wireless, dedicated links).
	Enterprise telecom solutions (VoIP, VPN, connectivity solutions).
	Stay updated on industry trends and competitor offerings.


Proposal &amp; Documentation:


	Develop detailed technical documentation and bills of quantities (BoQ).
	Assist in preparing RFP/RFQ responses.
	Ensure accuracy in solution costing and technical specifications.


Post-Sales Support:


	Assist in resolving client technical issues where necessary.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in a Telecoms Company, with focus on:
	
		Cloud services
		Internet and enterprise connectivity solutions
	
	
	Relevant certifications (e.g., Cloud, Networking) will be an added advantage.


Key Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong understanding of cloud computing and telecom infrastructure.
	Excellent communication and presentation skills.
	Ability to simplify complex technical concepts for clients.
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
	Customer-focused with a commercial mindset.
	Ability to work collaboratively with sales teams.


Working Relationships:


	Sales &amp; Business Development Team
	Network/Technical Operations Team
	Customers and External Stakeholders


Additional Requirements:


	Willingness to attend frequent client meetings.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Strong attention to detail and professionalism.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to:&nbsp;careers@brainshare.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IP / Senior Engineer",
  "description": "Brainshare Technologies &amp; Services Nig. Ltd is a Telecom services provider. We offer connectivity, managed services and other value added services through our fiber optic infrastructure.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;IP / Senior Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos Island - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The IP / Senior Engineer (Switching) is responsible for the design, implementation, optimization, and support of the organization&rsquo;s IP network and switching infrastructure.
	The role requires deep technical expertise in routing and switching technologies, ensuring high availability, performance, and security of network services.


Key Responsibilities
Network Design &amp; Implementation:


	Design and deploy scalable IP network and switching solutions to support business operations.
	Configure and integrate Layer 2 and Layer 3 switching infrastructure.
	Support network expansion, upgrades, and new site deployments.


Network Operations &amp; Support:


	Monitor network performance and ensure maximum uptime and reliability.
	Troubleshoot and resolve complex network issues (L2/L3, VLANs, STP, routing protocols, etc.).
	Perform root cause analysis and implement preventive measures.


Switching &amp; Routing Management:


	Manage core, distribution, and access layer switches.
	Configure and maintain routing protocols such as OSPF, BGP, and static routing.
	Implement VLAN segmentation, trunking, and redundancy protocols (e.g., HSRP/VRRP).


Performance Optimization:


	Analyze network traffic and optimize performance for efficiency and scalability.
	Ensure QoS policies are implemented and maintained for critical services.


Network Security:


	Implement network security best practices including ACLs, port security, and segmentation.
	Support firewall and intrusion prevention integrations where required.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Maintain accurate network diagrams, configurations, and documentation.
	Provide regular reports on network performance, incidents, and improvements.


Collaboration &amp; Support:


	Work closely with NOC, field engineers, and other technical teams.
	Provide technical guidance and mentorship to junior engineers.
	Support cross-functional projects and initiatives.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Electrical / Electronics Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or related field.
	5&ndash;8 years of relevant experience in IP networking and switching.
	Professional certifications such as CCNP, CCIE, or equivalent are highly preferred.


Technical Skills:


	Strong knowledge of switching technologies (Cisco, Huawei, MikroTik, etc.).
	Proficiency in routing protocols (BGP, OSPF, EIGRP).
	Experience with VLANs, STP, VTP, trunking, and redundancy protocols.
	Knowledge of MPLS, VPNs, and IP addressing schemes.
	Familiarity with network monitoring tools (e.g., SolarWinds, PRTG).
	Understanding of network security principles.


Soft Skills:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and reporting ability.
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple tasks.
	Team player with leadership and mentoring capability.


Method of Application&nbsp;

title: Job Openings at INTERSOS Nigeria
content:
INTERSOS is an independent humanitarian organization that assists the victims of natural disasters, armed conflicts and exclusion. Its activities are based on the principles of solidarity, justice, human dignity, equality of rights and opportunities, respect for diversity and coexistence, paying special attention to the most vulnerable people.

1.) Senior Communication Officer

Job Location: Maiduguri, Borno

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Under the supervision of the Grants and Reporting Manager, the Senior Communications Officer will be responsible for the delivery of quality internal and external communications outcomes and outputs for enhancing our organizational profile and strengthening humanitarian advocacy among the public, partners, donors, and other stakeholders.
	The Senior Communications Officer shall develop and design publications and audio-visual materials on our operations and coordinate the organization of major events and campaigns.
	While the position should primarily support the new OCHA project, the Senior Communications Officer will be supporting other projects of the mission on an ad-hoc basis.


Responsibilities
In compliance with the NHF Communication and Visibility Plan:


	Act as the mission Communication Focal Point and coordinate communication focal persons across project locations to ensure timely production and submission of deliverables.
	Provide clear guidance to project teams on branding, attribution, and visibility requirements in line with NHF/OCHA standards.
	Oversee the planning, collection, and development of communication outputs (photos, testimonials, stories, and multimedia) in compliance with donor requirements, INTERSOS standards, and the Do No Harm principle.
	Coordinate with program teams to identify and plan communication opportunities aligned with project objectives and key milestones.
	Conduct regular field visits to support content collection, verify quality, and ensure adherence to ethical communication standards.
	Manage mission social media platforms, ensuring content is accurate, compliant, and aligned with visibility requirements.
	Ensure all communication materials follow a structured workflow, including content collection, validation at the country level, and timely submission to donors.
	Deliver training to staff on informed consent, ethical communication practices, and donor visibility requirements.


Additionally, the role involves providing ad hoc support for the following:

2.) Head of Program

Code: SR-49-10177
Job Location: Maiduguri, Borno
Starting date: 07/05/2026
Contract duration: 6 months
Reporting to: Country Director
Functional Supervisor: Deputy Regional Director
Supervision of: PMs, Technical Coordinators
Type of Duty Station: Non-family duty station

General Description of the Context


	With over a decade of conflict, there have been devastating humanitarian consequences across North-East (Borno, Adamawa, and Yobe (BAY) states) and the North-West of Nigeria. According to the Humanitarian Needs Overview 2023, an estimated 8.3 million women, men, and children need some form of humanitarian and protection assistance.
	Over 2 million people have been forced to flee their homes due to persistent violence and remain internally displaced. Food insecurity and malnutrition are exacerbated by inflation, poverty, and restricted access due to insecurity, which impedes livelihood opportunities.
	Gender-based violence (GBV) has been a significant protection concern faced by IDP women and girls in the area. Numerous basic infrastructures, such as hospitals, clinics, and water supplies, are damaged, posing constraints on essential service provision. Epidemics like cholera arising from flooding and overcrowded settings put extra stress on the already vulnerable communities.


General Purpose of the Position


	The Head of Program is responsible for the overall program strategy across the country and for providing technical input to the team within the different sectors.
	S/he is also responsible for developing policies, processes and standards within the respective area of responsibility.
	S/he will enable and oversee the monitoring and evaluation of the activities across the country and s/he is finally responsible for the development of the portfolio, ensuring consistent donors reporting, internal and external representation.
	Ultimately, s/he will be active in high level and strategic decision-making, collaborating closely with the Country Director and the Finance and Logistics Managers.


Main Responsibilities and Tasks
Strategy and vision:


	Contribute to the development and update of the country strategy, within INTERSOS overall global strategic plan, values and principles
	Work as part of the Senior Management Team to share ideas and program recommendations, supporting and implementing continuous improvement on activities, processes and procedures to optimise results and improve quality of delivery in line with quality standards
	Determine, set and deliver goals and objectives for the program team in line with the mission strategic objectives
	Develop in collaboration with technical referents, high quality program strategies and proposals in relevance with context and needs
	Ensure that program strategies and processes are in place to meet quality implementation and to achieve the targets.
	Develop holistic and needs-based programs, identifying funding opportunities and strategic partnerships.
	Design and roll-out creative and innovative programming, lead assessments for rapid response or scale-up programming in existing or new areas of intervention
	Together with the Country Director and the Finance and Logistics Managers, create and implement best practice program management vision, strategy, policies, processes and procedures to aid and improve organizational performance
	Supervise the design of standards and processes with technical referents at Mission level.


Program management:


	Supervise and guide the program team (Program Managers, Grants & Reporting Managers, Technical Coordinators)
	Ensure program coordination and coherence across portfolio
	Ensure program and project reviews are regularly and effectively conducted, monitor the delivery of programs based on work plan
	Together with the Finance and Logistics Managers, ensure adequate resources allocation are provisioned to deliver programs.
	Trigger and oversee the design of concept notes, proposals and budgets for cost-related activities, ensuring the coordination with Program Managers, Grants & Reporting Manager, Technical Units and support functions (HR, Finance, Logistics and Security), in line with INTERSOS and donors’ requirements
	Ensure timely communication and information flow on program achievements to internal and external parties
	Conduct risk assessments of the program activities and related processes


Accountability and quality control:


	Oversee and support continuous improvement of program quality standards
	Explore innovative solutions to improve the impact on program
	Oversee the program development, implementation, ensuring targets and quality are respected in collaboration with MEAL department
	Ensure the appropriate systems and procedures are established and followed, ensuring best practices in project management are developed and adopted with appropriate program management frameworks in place


Representation and advocacy:


	Together with the Country Director and Finance and Logistics Managers, establish strong and efficient partnerships with organisations, donors and authorities, in line with the country strategy
	Represent INTERSOS in relevant coordination forums
	Contribute to the definition of the country advocacy strategy and represent INTERSOS within national and international media and participate in community activities as appropriate
	Upon delegation of authority, act as Country Director when needed


People management:

	Communicate with the program team on program status, risks and issues in an appropriate and timely manner. Ensure regular and timely communication between project team members
	Monitor the completion of tasks and ensure good performance and record on appropriate systems
	Motivate and coach the program team. Communicate key performance Indicators (KPIs) to the staffs so that they are aware of the strategic annual plan
	Consistently promote high standards through personal example and roll out through the team so that each member understands the standards and the behaviours expected
	In collaboration with the HR Coordinator, undertake continuous training and staff development. Inspire the team and provide technical expertise
	In collaboration with the Protection and/or Medical coordinator, support the identification and deployment of the technical skill sets, resource levels and systems to deliver projects, including the engagement of external resources as required


Requirements

	Negotiation and networking skills

	Innovation and creativity
